verses.text: When the LORD shall bring thee into the land — cast out seven nations greater and mightier than thou. Smite them, utterly destroy them; make no covenant, shew no mercy. Neither make marriages with them — for they will turn away thy son from following Me. Destroy their altars, break their images, cut down their groves. Guidance: A hard chapter for any age. The seven nations were not just neighbours; they were the cultic systems that ate their own children at altars. Israel's holiness was fragile; intermarriage and tolerated idols would dissolve it within a generation. The danger is not the foreign spouse — it is the spouse's gods.

For thou art an holy people unto the LORD thy God: the LORD thy God hath chosen thee to be a special people unto Himself, above all people upon the face of the earth. Guidance: 'Holy' means set apart, not better. God chose Israel the way a craftsman picks one stone from a quarry — not because it shone, but because He saw what He would make of it. Identity precedes obedience: HOLY first, then DO.

The LORD did not set His love upon you because ye were more in number — ye were the fewest of all people. But because the LORD loved you, and because He would keep the oath He sware unto your fathers, the LORD brought you out with a mighty hand, redeemed you out of the house of bondmen, from the hand of Pharaoh. Guidance: He chose you because He chose you. The most circular sentence in the Torah and the most reassuring. Love that is grounded in itself, not in your numbers or your usefulness — that is love that can outlast every season.

Know therefore that the LORD thy God, He is God, the faithful God, which keepeth covenant and mercy with them that love Him and keep His commandments to a thousand generations; and repayeth them that hate Him to their face, to destroy them. Guidance: The Hebrew word is hesed — covenant love that does not let go. A thousand generations of mercy; punishment to the face of those who hate Him. He does not work through middlemen for either blessing or judgment. He keeps His own promises and pays His own debts.

If ye hearken, keep these judgments, the LORD shall keep the covenant He sware to thy fathers. He will love thee, bless thee, multiply thee — fruit of thy womb, fruit of thy land, corn, wine, oil, herds. Not be male or female barren among you. The LORD will take away all sickness. Serve not their gods, for that will be a snare. Guidance: Love translated into wombs, wheat, oil, herds. God's blessing reaches the body and the barn. The warning is small but lethal: their gods will become snares. Even after victory, the trap can spring.

If thou shalt say in thine heart, these nations are more than I; how can I dispossess them? Be not afraid: remember what the LORD did unto Pharaoh — the signs, wonders, mighty hand, stretched out arm. So shall the LORD do to all the people whom thou fearest. He will send the hornet. Be not affrighted, for the LORD thy God is among you, a mighty God and terrible. Guidance: Fear gets named, then answered. The reply to 'they are too many' is 'remember Egypt.' Memory of past deliverance is the antidote to present dread. And then there are the hornets — God's smallest soldiers, who can rout cities for Him.

The LORD will put out those nations by little and little. Thou mayest not consume them at once, lest the beasts of the field increase. Burn the graven images with fire; not desire the silver or gold on them, nor take it, lest thou be snared. Bring not an abomination into thine house, lest thou be a cursed thing like it: utterly detest, utterly abhor it. Guidance: Little by little — God paces the victory to fit His people's growth. Faster would breed wolves before the farmers were ready. Burn the gold-plated idols and don't pocket the gold. What looks like resource is often a curse with a price tag.
